ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for deep-fried gluten-and-soy mock chicken; FOODON_03315595 (meat analog food product) is the closest defensible broader class.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for mock chicken / meat analog in the clinical terminology; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of mock chicken is approximately 100 g, equivalent to a palm-sized portion, commonly served as a protein dish alongside rice or noodles in a Singaporean meal.

nutrient_source.title: Mock Chicken — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mock Chicken (CR ID: F06020005). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical Singapore source at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Soy-Gluten Meat Analog — Analogous estimate based on wheat gluten and soy protein composite foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for deep-fried mock chicken exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 40 (low) based on the composite profile of wheat gluten (GI ~54 for wheat gluten bread analogs) and soy protein isolate (GI ~18 per University of Sydney data for soy-based foods), weighted by the dominant protein-and-fibre matrix of this product. Carbohydrate content is low (6.51 g/100g), so glycemic load per 100 g serving = (40 × 6.51) / 100 = 2.6 g, classified as low GL. This estimate is conservative and consistent with published data on high-protein, moderate-fibre plant-based meat analogs.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 389 mg per 100 g — borderline high relative to AHA DASH threshold of 200 mg per 100 g for individual food items
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit mock chicken to small portions (50–75 g) and avoid additional high-sodium sauces or seasonings, as the 389 mg sodium per 100 g approaches the upper limit recommended by AHA DASH dietary guidelines; choose steamed or braised preparations over deep-fried to avoid compounding sodium from frying oils and coatings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (6.51 g/100g), low sugar (2.0 g/100g), high protein (16.67 g/100g), and moderate dietary fibre (2.28 g/100g) support glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mock chicken is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate load (estimated GL 2.6 per 100 g serving) and high protein content that slows gastric emptying and blunts postprandial glucose rise; however, patients should verify ingredient labels for added starch or maltodextrin, which can elevate the effective GI, and should prefer non-fried preparations per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 389 mg/100g is elevated; saturated fat is low (~2.1 g/100g) and cholesterol is negligible (plant-based product), which is favourable, but deep-frying adds total fat and potential oxidised lipids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Mock chicken presents a mixed cardiovascular profile: its plant-based protein, low saturated fat (~2.1 g/100g), and zero cholesterol are consistent with AHA heart-healthy dietary patterns, but the elevated sodium (389 mg/100g) and deep-fried preparation increase cardiovascular risk; individuals with CVD should choose low-sodium variants, limit portion size to 75 g, and prefer steamed or baked preparations to reduce total fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium (389 mg/100g) may exacerbate RA-related tissue oedema; plant-based protein and soy isoflavones offer mild anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may consume mock chicken in moderation given its plant-based protein and soy isoflavone content, but should select low-sodium variants and avoid deep-fried preparations, as high sodium (389 mg/100g) can worsen RA-associated tissue swelling and the absence of omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, or other established anti-inflammatory nutrients means this food does not actively support RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mock chicken is a processed plant-based meat alternative made primarily from wheat gluten (seitan) and soybean fibres, deep-fried to produce a firm, chewy texture that mimics the mouthfeel of chicken. It is widely consumed in Singapore and across Chinese-speaking communities in Southeast Asia as a vegetarian and vegan protein substitute, commonly served in hawker centres, Buddhist vegetarian restaurants, and home cooking. The product is typically seasoned with soy sauce, five-spice powder, and other savoury flavourings before or after frying, resulting in a savoury, umami-rich taste with a slightly crispy exterior. Nutritionally, mock chicken provides 204 kcal per 100 g, with a high protein content of 16.67 g, moderate fat of 12.29 g (predominantly from frying oil), and low carbohydrate of 6.51 g, making it a high-protein, low-carbohydrate food suitable for glycaemic management. Dietary fibre is 2.28 g per 100 g and sodium is 389 mg per 100 g, the latter being a clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The food is halal-certified in many commercial preparations, plant-based, and free of animal cholesterol. It contains gluten and soy allergens and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or soy allergy. In clinical nutrition contexts, mock chicken is a useful high-protein plant-based option for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low glycaemic load, but sodium content requires monitoring for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F06020005) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from typical deep-fried gluten-soy product profiles consistent with USDA FoodData Central analogous entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for soy and gluten composite foods, documented transparently. Multilingual names verified against standard Chinese culinary terminology (素鸡) and Malay/Tamil/Hindi translations for Singapore context.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific HPB mock chicken entry is not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of April 2026. USDA FoodData Central does not contain an exact match for this Singapore-specific product. 2. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific HPB mock chicken entry are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this Singapore product formulation as of April 2026. 3. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0: plant-based deep-fried products using vegetable oils typically contain negligible industrial trans fats; confirmed as trace/negligible per HPB dietary guidance for vegetable-oil-fried products. 4. cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0: mock chicken is a fully plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients; cholesterol is naturally zero. 5. saturated_fat_g of 2.1 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for deep-fried wheat gluten and soy protein products (closest match: USDA SR Legacy item for seitan/wheat gluten, adapted for frying oil contribution). 6. GI value of 40 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Halal dietary tag is preserved from input; verification of specific brand halal certification is recommended at point of purchase.
